spec_match.rationale: The page now uses the draft EVP surface: an email input with autocomplete=email, a session nonce, and an emailverified event listener for the browser-supplied presentation token. It separately labels the local server sample as a demo backend path.

interactive.rationale: The typed email and generated nonce now drive the local signing and validation calls, token claims, and validation checklist. Native browser click-through remains unverified because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable in this session.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept now explains the actual relying-party page surface, the browser-mediated token event, and the fallback path when the native API is unavailable.

edge_cases.rationale: The page handles missing native EVP support, insecure contexts, missing presentation tokens, backend failures, fresh nonce generation, audience/nonce validation, and replay rejection through the conformance route.

references.rationale: The page links to the ChromeStatus feature entry and the WICG Email Verification Protocol draft.

design_system.rationale: The replacement layout uses minmax(0, ...) grids, min-width:0 cards, scrollable/wrapping code and token boxes, responsive one-column breakpoints, and shared design-system variables.

openQuestions.title: Verify native EVP flow in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp in a Chrome 150/Canary profile with EVP enabled to open the page, confirm the native support banner is accurate, focus the email input, observe whether the browser fires emailverified, and verify the local server sample layout does not overflow on desktop or mobile widths.

summary: Replaced the pretend timed walkthrough with a native EVP event listener plus explicit feature detection and a real local backend sample/validation path. Browser verification is still pending because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable.
